extracted_source_families.magnitude_semantics: 1000-year recurrence-period geodetic scenario family, not a deterministic forecast
extracted_source_families.rake_semantics: constant most-hazardous slip-angle assumption in the PTHA
extracted_source_families.source_location: Methods, Potential tsunami scenario set and Probabilistic assessment method

extracted_generation_contract.released_dataset_contents: target-point tsunami height, recurrence and wave-height/period distributions; not complete per-scenario PEGS source records

liu_2023_extraction_contract.individual_longitude_latitude_strike_dip: available in source Tables 4 and 6; not promoted to PEGS records
liu_2023_extraction_contract.arrival_values_semantics: approximate narrative ranges, including equal endpoints where the source says approximately one value
liu_2023_extraction_contract.arrival_definition: modelled tsunami-wave arrival at named coastal observation points; threshold definition not stated in extracted text

manila_specific_evidence.scope: kinematic rupture sensitivity for Manila-trench tsunami propagation
manila_specific_evidence.reason_not_promoted: The accessible primary record establishes a Manila sensitivity study but does not expose an auditable event-specific rupture-velocity table.

rupture_velocity.general_source_semantics: finite-fault inversion sensitivity values; not a Manila prior or posterior

rise_time.source_semantics: 2011 Tohoku sensitivity range quoted by a Manila kinematic-tsunami study; not transferable as a Manila source prior without a declared scaling model

dynamic_parameter_literature_audit.allowed_current_use: The cited numeric values may define explicitly labelled external sensitivity branches only. They may not fill the missing Manila scenario fields or support operational warning claims.
dynamic_parameter_literature_audit.remaining_evidence_gate: Obtain an auditable Manila-specific finite-fault source or pre-register a defensible scaling law with uncertainty before PEGS production registration.

sources.title: 马尼拉俯冲带潜在地震海啸对华南沿海的影响

sources.verified_scope: A north-Manila-trench tsunami generally affects Hong Kong and southeast China after more than about three hours; this is not a scenario-specific numeric arrival.

sources.title: Possible Maximum Earthquake Tsunami Along the South Coast of China Inferred From GPS-Derived Surface Velocities

Effect of kinematic fault rupture process on tsunami propagation
The 1991--2015 series of large interplate earthquakes: Slower slip at the plate interface for larger events

required_before_scenario_registration: exact segment geometry and coordinate convention
Mw, strike, dip, rake and top depth
rupture length, width, slip, rise time and rupture velocity or explicit missing status
explicit separation of recurrence-family magnitude from deterministic event prediction
no conversion of qualitative three-plus-hours into an exact universal constant
